subject: Workbench projection root test large-file reduction
questions: Whether the split lost, duplicated, misplaced, or weakened projection test assertions.
Whether the shared helper introduced cross-test mutable state, path errors, or unreasonable coupling.
Whether the manifest and stateful plan honestly keep the root file as planned_refactor with a compliant next target.
Whether the split created new near-threshold or large-file bypass risk.

blocking_findings.summary: The plan marked LFA-P5.3 as pass while its acceptance text still said full large-file and closeout gates were pending.

repairs.summary: Changed LFA-P5.3 to in_progress until full gates complete.
Split all newly extracted projection shards below 300 lines and reran the large-file gate against staged files.
Changed helper task_dag construction to a shallow copy and removed unused createRunManifest imports from two shard files.

non_blocking_findings.summary: The helper shallow-copies work package objects but not nested arrays. DeepSeek assessed this as non-blocking because current fixture shapes are shallow and tests do not mutate nested work package data in place.

evidence: Root projection test file is 1434 lines, down from the 3175-line baseline.
All extracted projection shards are below 300 lines.
Manifest keeps test/workbench-projection.test.js as planned_refactor with target_lines 1184 and minimum_reduction 250.
Plan keeps LFA-P5 in review_pending during review and does not mark full gates pass before they run.
DeepSeek delta review found no blocking behavior, import, or shared-state risk in the post-PASS cleanup.

local_gate_evidence: npm run check:large-files passed with staged files and no warnings.
npm run check:closeout passed after installing ignored root and Workbench app dependencies in the isolated worktree.
